rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE BETTY DUGAN FAMILY TRUST AS RESTATED ON APRIL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Respondents Demurrer And Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is specially-appearing Respondent Jodi Birenbaum's Demurrer and Motion to Change Venue. Per the Notice of Motion, Respondent demurs to Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee is brought on the grounds that Petitioner Sharon Wilson lacks standing. The Notice and moving papers also argue this court is not the correct venue for the Petition, as the trust is administered elsewhere. The Notice states "venue is proper in the State of Oregon" while the Memorandum of Points and Authorities states "Maricopa County in Chandler, Arizona is the Proper Venue." Counsel's declaration in support of the Demurrer states the trustee resides in Portland, Oregon and the administrative attorney maintains an office in Chandler, Arizona, and seeks an order transferring this matter to Superior Court of Maricopa County, Arizona (Grant Decl. par. 4, 8). No opposition to the Demurrer has been filed. No Notice of Non-Opposition has been filed. A hearing date for the Demurrer was approved on August 13, 2025, in accordance with Local Rule 14.10. A hearing date on a motion to change venue was not given in accordance with Local Rule 14.10. The portion of the pleading that seeks an order transferring venue is therefore DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE to being filed as a separate motion, on a hearing date obtained pursuant to the procedure described in Local Rule 14.10. The Demurrer to Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee on the grounds of lack of standing and improper venue is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Petitioner Sharon Wilson may file an amended petition, within ten (10) days after entry of this Order. Respondents shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
